title: Traffic Light with Scoped Enum
prompt_md:
## Goal
Model a traffic light with a scoped enumeration (`enum class`) and report both the color name and the action a driver should take.

Light codes: `0` = Red, `1` = Yellow, `2` = Green. The action for each is Stop, Caution, Go respectively.

## Input
- A single integer.

## Output
One line:
```
<Color>: <Action>
```
For a valid code (0, 1, or 2) print the matching color and action. For any other input print:
```
Unknown: Invalid
```

## Constraints
- Use a scoped enumeration (`enum class Light`); all enumerator access must use scope resolution (e.g. `Light::red`). Convert the input code with `static_cast`.

title: Permission Checker with Explicit Underlying Type
prompt_md:
## Goal
Model Unix-style file permissions with a scoped enumeration that has an explicit underlying type, then report which permissions are present in a combined permission value.

The permission bits are: read = 1, write = 2, execute = 4. A combined value adds the bits together (e.g. `5` = read + execute, `7` = all three, `0` = none).

## Input
- A single integer in the range 0-7.

## Output
Exactly three lines:
```
Read: <yes/no>
Write: <yes/no>
Execute: <yes/no>
```
Print `yes` if that permission's bit is set in the input value, otherwise `no`.

## Constraints
- Define `enum class Permission` with an explicit underlying type and the bit values above.
- Determine whether a bit is set using the enumerator's underlying value (`static_cast<int>`) and a bitwise AND; do not special-case each of the eight possible inputs.

test_cases.label: Sample -- all permissions (7)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: yes
Write: yes
Execute: yes

test_cases.label: Hidden -- read and execute only (5)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: yes
Write: no
Execute: yes

test_cases.label: Hidden -- read only (1)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: yes
Write: no
Execute: no

test_cases.label: Hidden -- write only (2)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: no
Write: yes
Execute: no

test_cases.label: Hidden -- read and write (3)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: yes
Write: yes
Execute: no

test_cases.label: Hidden -- execute only (4)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: no
Write: no
Execute: yes

test_cases.label: Hidden -- write and execute (6)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: no
Write: yes
Execute: yes

test_cases.label: Hidden -- no permissions (0)
test_cases.expected_stdout:
Read: no
Write: no
Execute: no
